_meta.description: REAL capture, converted to DetectionEvent shapes. Do not hand-edit the events without updating this file's source pointers below.
_meta.video: iYlODtkyw_I — MrBeast, ~35 min (spike/RESULTS.md §2 row 2, rewatched ≥3x, 'green (high)' by ground truth)
_meta.session: logged-in, rewatch (this browser had already watched this exact video ≥3-4 times)
_meta.note: spike/RESULTS.md §3 finding 5: rewatch-frequency capping stripped ALL placements server-side for this session, even though the video is a confirmed green (the SAME video, watched fresh and logged-out minutes later in the same export, shows the full '1/18/1' signature — see green-mrbeast-iYlODtkyw_I.json). This is the real-data proof that absence-of-evidence on a rewatch is worthless: CLAUDE.md invariant 5 and classify()'s 'recent-rewatch' NO_SIGNAL cause exist specifically to keep this from being misread as NO_ADS.

sourceRecords.note: Confirmation read, same result: adPlacements empty.

omittedFromExport: No dom_ad_event records exist for this session in the raw export (verified: zero DOM records timestamped before 2026-07-02T20:38:00Z, when the logged-out/fresh session for the same video begins) — 'none' DOM ads played, matching RESULTS.md's 'absent' / 'none' row exactly. Nothing was trimmed here; there is simply no DOM evidence to include.

Fetch-path noise immediately preceding the 'initial' read (pageVideoId=iYlODtkyw_I but a different/absent videoId), at 2026-07-02T20:34:11.457Z-20:34:11.468Z: three records with videoId=null and note='no videoDetails' (malformed/prefetch reads with no videoDetails at all), one with videoId='ux' (2-character garbage from a non-canonical sibling endpoint — the /youtubei/v1/player/ad_break-class issue classifier.ts's summarizePlacementCounts doc comment describes), and one with an 11-character videoId (hQUUT8qHIxJ) that is an ad creative's OWN player response through the identical /youtubei/v1/player endpoint. All real but exactly what the real pipeline drops: classify() and background.ts's handleIncomingEvent both filter on videoId !== pageVideoId (or a missing videoId) before an event would ever reach a session — omitted here for the identical reason, not fabricated absence.
conversionNotes: No BEACON events captured by the Phase 0 spike tool (see green-mrbeast-iYlODtkyw_I.json).
_meta.expectedClassification: Absence of ANY evidence (no placements, no DOM, no beacon). With ClassifierContext.recentlyWatched=true (this browser watched this exact videoId recently) → NO_SIGNAL 'recent-rewatch', NEVER NO_ADS (CLAUDE.md invariant 5). With recentlyWatched=false + a valid observer, the SAME zero-evidence events would classify NO_ADS instead — demonstrating why the rewatch flag is load-bearing and not just documentation.
